Title: Start a new visit for an active patient with a valid visit type
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician at a session location; patient record exists with no active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Click Start Visit | 3. Select a visit type | 4. Confirm the visit start datetime defaults to now | 5. Click Start Visit
Expected_Result: An active visit is created, the dashboard shows an Active Visit banner, and the visit appears in the active visits list

Title: Prevent starting a second active visit when one is already open
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient already has an active visit at the same location
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Observe the existing Active Visit banner | 3. Attempt to click Start Visit
Expected_Result: The system blocks creating a concurrent active visit and informs the user a visit is already active for this patient

Title: Start visit requires a visit type to be selected
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has no active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Click Start Visit | 3. Leave visit type unselected | 4. Click Start Visit
Expected_Result: A validation error is shown and the visit is not created until a visit type is selected

Title: Start visit datetime cannot be set in the future
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has no active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Click Start Visit | 3. Set start datetime to tomorrow | 4. Click Start Visit
Expected_Result: A validation error is shown stating the visit cannot start in the future and the visit is not created

Title: Start visit at exactly the current timestamp boundary
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has no active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Click Start Visit | 3. Set start datetime to the current minute | 4. Click Start Visit
Expected_Result: The visit is created successfully with a start datetime equal to the current time

Title: Add a retrospective past visit within allowed date range
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician with retrospective entry privilege; patient has no overlapping visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Click Add Past Visit | 3. Select a visit type | 4. Set start date in the past | 5. Set stop date after start | 6. Save
Expected_Result: A closed past visit is created with the specified start and stop dates and appears in visit history

Title: Reject past visit where stop date precedes start date
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient record exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Click Add Past Visit | 3. Set start date | 4. Set stop date before start date | 5. Save
Expected_Result: A validation error is shown that stop date must be after start date and the visit is not saved

Title: Reject past visit with start date before patient birthdate
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient DOB is 15-Jan-1990
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Click Add Past Visit | 3. Set start date before DOB | 4. Save
Expected_Result: A validation error is shown that visit cannot start before the patient was born and the visit is not saved

Title: Past visit stop date on the boundary equal to start date
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient record exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Click Add Past Visit | 3. Set start datetime | 4. Set stop datetime equal to start datetime | 5. Save
Expected_Result: System behavior is validated: either accepts a zero-length visit or rejects per configured rule, consistently and without server error

Title: End an active visit successfully
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Locate the Active Visit banner | 3. Click End Visit | 4. Confirm the action
Expected_Result: The visit is closed with a stop datetime set, the Active Visit banner is removed, and the visit moves to history

Title: Ended visit no longer appears in active visits list
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active visit shown in active visits list
Test_Steps: 1. Open active visits list | 2. Note the patient visit | 3. End the visit from the dashboard | 4. Refresh active visits list
Expected_Result: The patient visit is removed from the active visits list after it is ended

Title: Stop datetime cannot precede the visit start datetime
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician with privilege to edit visit stop datetime; patient has an active visit started today
Test_Steps: 1. Open the visit details | 2. Edit the stop datetime | 3. Set stop before start | 4. Save
Expected_Result: A validation error is shown that stop datetime must be on or after start datetime and the change is rejected

Title: End visit blocked when stop datetime is in the future
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open the visit details | 2. Set stop datetime to a future time | 3. Save
Expected_Result: A validation error is shown that visit cannot end in the future and the change is rejected

Title: Active visits list shows all currently open visits at a location
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; at least two patients have active visits at the session location
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to Active Visits / Find Patient with active visits | 2. Review the list
Expected_Result: All patients with active visits at the location are listed with patient name, visit type, and start time

Title: Search active visits by patient name
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; multiple active visits exist
Test_Steps: 1. Open Active Visits list | 2. Type a patient name fragment in the search box | 3. Review filtered results
Expected_Result: Only active visits matching the search term are displayed

Title: Active visits list is empty when no visits are open
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; no patient has an active visit at the location
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to Active Visits list
Expected_Result: An empty-state message is shown and no visits are listed

Title: Create a new custom visit type as administrator
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator with Manage Visit Types privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to Administration | 2. Open Manage Visit Types | 3. Click Add Visit Type | 4. Enter name and description | 5. Save
Expected_Result: The new visit type is created and becomes selectable when starting a visit

Title: Reject duplicate visit type name
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator; a visit type named Facility Visit already exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open Manage Visit Types | 2. Click Add Visit Type | 3. Enter a name that already exists | 4. Save
Expected_Result: A validation error is shown that the visit type name must be unique and the duplicate is not created

Title: Retire a visit type and confirm it is hidden from selection
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator; a non-essential visit type exists and is unused
Test_Steps: 1. Open Manage Visit Types | 2. Select the visit type | 3. Click Retire and enter a reason | 4. Save | 5. Start a new visit and review available types
Expected_Result: The retired visit type is no longer offered when starting a new visit but remains on historical visits

Title: Create a clinical encounter within an active visit
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open the active visit | 2. Start a clinical form (e.g. Vitals) | 3. Enter encounter datetime within the visit window | 4. Enter observations | 5. Save
Expected_Result: An encounter is created and linked to the active visit, and observations are recorded

Title: Encounter datetime must fall within the parent visit window
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has a visit with start 09:00 and stop 11:00
Test_Steps: 1. Open the visit | 2. Create an encounter | 3. Set encounter datetime outside the visit window | 4. Save
Expected_Result: A validation error is shown that encounter datetime must be within the visit period and the encounter is not saved

Title: Encounter datetime exactly at visit start boundary is accepted
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has a visit starting at 09:00
Test_Steps: 1. Open the visit | 2. Create an encounter | 3. Set encounter datetime equal to visit start | 4. Save
Expected_Result: The encounter is accepted with datetime equal to the visit start boundary

Title: Encounter datetime cannot be in the future
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open the visit | 2. Create an encounter | 3. Set encounter datetime to a future time | 4. Save
Expected_Result: A validation error is shown that encounter datetime cannot be in the future and it is not saved

Title: Add a single provider with a role to an encounter
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active visit; provider record exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the encounter form | 2. Add a provider | 3. Assign the encounter role | 4. Save
Expected_Result: The encounter is saved with the provider and role displayed in encounter details

Title: Add multiple providers with distinct roles to one encounter
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; multiple provider records exist
Test_Steps: 1. Open the encounter form | 2. Add first provider as Clinician | 3. Add second provider as Nurse | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Both providers with their respective roles are saved and shown in encounter details

Title: Encounter requires at least one provider when configured as mandatory
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; encounter type configured to require a provider
Test_Steps: 1. Open the encounter form | 2. Do not add any provider | 3. Save
Expected_Result: A validation error is shown that a provider is required and the encounter is not saved

Title: Remove a provider from an existing encounter
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; encounter exists with two providers
Test_Steps: 1. Open the encounter | 2. Edit providers | 3. Remove one provider | 4. Save
Expected_Result: The removed provider no longer appears and the remaining provider is retained with audit of the change

Title: Reject a new past visit that overlaps an existing visit
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an existing visit 01-Jun 09:00 to 01-Jun 17:00
Test_Steps: 1. Click Add Past Visit | 2. Set start and stop overlapping the existing visit | 3. Save
Expected_Result: A validation error is shown that visits cannot overlap for the same patient and the visit is not created

Title: Adjacent visits sharing a boundary timestamp are allowed
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has a visit ending 01-Jun 17:00
Test_Steps: 1. Click Add Past Visit | 2. Set start equal to the prior visit stop | 3. Set a later stop | 4. Save
Expected_Result: The adjacent non-overlapping visit is accepted per configured boundary rule without error

Title: Decision table for visit overlap acceptance across boundary conditions
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; existing visit A spans 09:00 to 17:00 on 01-Jun
Test_Steps: 1. For each combination of new start and stop, attempt to add the past visit | 2. Record whether the system accepts or rejects | 3. Compare against expected overlap matrix
Test_Data: Cases: before-A; after-A; contained-in-A; contains-A; partial-front; partial-back
Expected_Result: Visits fully outside A are accepted, all genuine overlaps are rejected, and behavior matches the documented overlap decision table

Title: Merge two visits for the same patient into one
Preconditions: Logged in as user with Merge Visits privilege; patient has two separate same-day visits with encounters
Test_Steps: 1. Open Manage Visits for the patient | 2. Select two visits | 3. Choose Merge | 4. Confirm target visit | 5. Save
Expected_Result: The two visits are merged, all encounters are reassigned to the surviving visit, and no encounters are lost

Title: Merging visits preserves all encounter datetimes and observations
Preconditions: Logged in as user with Merge Visits privilege; two visits each have encounters with observations
Test_Steps: 1. Record encounter datetimes and obs counts in both visits | 2. Merge the visits | 3. Review the surviving visit encounters
Expected_Result: The merged visit contains all three encounters with original datetimes and observation values intact

Title: Cannot merge visits belonging to different patients
Preconditions: Logged in as user with Merge Visits privilege; two visits belong to two different patients
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to select visits from two different patients | 2. Choose Merge
Expected_Result: The system prevents merging visits across patients and shows an error preserving each patient record separately

Title: Create a visit note encounter with provider and diagnosis
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open the active visit | 2. Click Visit Note | 3. Enter the clinical note text | 4. Add a primary diagnosis | 5. Set provider | 6. Save
Expected_Result: A visit note encounter is saved within the visit with note, diagnosis, and provider visible on the dashboard

Title: Add primary and secondary diagnoses to a visit note
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open Visit Note | 2. Add a primary diagnosis | 3. Add a secondary diagnosis | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Both diagnoses are saved with correct primary and secondary certainty and shown on the visit note

Title: Edit an existing visit note and verify version history
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; a saved visit note encounter exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the visit note | 2. Edit the note text | 3. Save | 4. Review encounter audit / change history
Expected_Result: The visit note is updated, the new text is shown, and the change is auditable with editor and timestamp

Title: State transitions of a visit from started to ended
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has no active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Start a visit (Not started to Active) | 2. Add an encounter (Active with data) | 3. End the visit (Active to Ended) | 4. Attempt to add an encounter to the ended visit
Expected_Result: Each transition behaves correctly and adding encounters to an ended visit is either blocked or warns per configuration

Title: Reopen or extend an ended visit if privilege allows
Preconditions: Logged in as user with edit-visit privilege; patient has an ended visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open the ended visit details | 2. Clear or extend the stop datetime | 3. Save | 4. Verify visit returns to active or remains closed per rules
Expected_Result: The system applies the configured rule consistently and records who reopened the visit and when

Title: Pairwise coverage of visit type, location, and start mode combinations
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician with access to multiple locations and visit types
Test_Steps: 1. For each pairwise combination of visit type, location, and start mode, start a visit | 2. Verify creation succeeds and attributes are stored
Expected_Result: Each pairwise combination creates a valid visit with the correct type, location, and start mode recorded

Title: Exploratory session on rapid start and end of visits and back navigation
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; test patient available
Test_Steps: 1. Rapidly start and end visits | 2. Use browser back button mid-flow | 3. Double-click Start Visit | 4. Refresh during save | 5. Observe for duplicates, orphan encounters, or errors
Expected_Result: No duplicate visits, orphaned encounters, or unhandled errors occur; any anomalies are documented with reproduction steps

Title: Unauthorized role cannot start or end visits
Preconditions: Logged in as a user without visit management privileges (e.g. read-only role)
Test_Steps: 1. Open a patient dashboard | 2. Attempt to access Start Visit and End Visit actions
Expected_Result: Visit management actions are hidden or denied and a direct attempt returns an authorization error

Title: Direct REST call to create a visit without authentication is rejected
Preconditions: REST API base URL is reachable; no session cookie or basic auth provided
Test_Steps: 1. Send a POST to /ws/rest/v1/visit without credentials | 2. Inspect the HTTP response
Expected_Result: The server returns 401 Unauthorized and no visit is created

Title: Visit and encounter actions are recorded in the audit log
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; audit logging enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Start a visit | 2. Create an encounter | 3. End the visit | 4. Review the audit/log table or admin audit view
Expected_Result: Each action is captured with user, timestamp, and entity reference for accountability

Title: Create a visit via OpenMRS REST API with valid payload
Preconditions: Authenticated REST session as Clinician; valid patient UUID and visit type UUID available
Test_Steps: 1. POST to /ws/rest/v1/visit with patient, visitType, and startDatetime | 2. Capture response | 3. GET the created visit
Expected_Result: HTTP 201 is returned with a visit UUID, and the GET confirms the stored attributes

Title: Reject visit creation via REST with invalid visit type UUID
Preconditions: Authenticated REST session as Clinician
Test_Steps: 1. POST to /ws/rest/v1/visit with a non-existent visitType UUID | 2. Inspect the response
Expected_Result: HTTP 400 or 404 is returned with an error message and no visit is created

Title: Stop a visit via REST by setting stopDatetime
Preconditions: Authenticated REST session; an active visit UUID exists
Test_Steps: 1. POST to /ws/rest/v1/visit/{uuid} with stopDatetime | 2. GET the visit | 3. Verify stopDatetime is persisted
Expected_Result: HTTP 200 is returned and the visit shows the stopDatetime, confirming closure

Title: Create encounter via REST linked to a visit
Preconditions: Authenticated REST session; valid visit UUID, encounterType UUID, and provider UUID available
Test_Steps: 1. POST to /ws/rest/v1/encounter with visit, encounterType, encounterDatetime, and encounterProviders | 2. GET the encounter
Expected_Result: HTTP 201 is returned and the encounter is linked to the visit with the provider recorded

Title: Retrieve a visit as a FHIR Encounter resource
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; a visit exists mapped to a FHIR Encounter
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/Encounter/{id} | 2. Validate the resource against the FHIR R4 Encounter profile
Expected_Result: A valid FHIR R4 Encounter is returned with status, class, subject, and period populated correctly

Title: FHIR Encounter status maps correctly to OpenMRS visit state
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; one active and one ended visit exist
Test_Steps: 1. GET the FHIR Encounter for an active visit | 2. GET the FHIR Encounter for an ended visit | 3. Compare status values
Expected_Result: Active visit maps to status in-progress and ended visit maps to status finished per FHIR mapping rules

Title: FHIR Encounter period reflects visit start and stop datetimes
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; a closed visit with known start and stop exists
Test_Steps: 1. GET the FHIR Encounter resource | 2. Inspect Encounter.period.start and Encounter.period.end
Expected_Result: Encounter.period.start equals visit start and period.end equals visit stop in correct ISO8601 with timezone

Title: FHIR Encounter participant maps encounter providers
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; an encounter with two providers exists
Test_Steps: 1. GET the FHIR Encounter resource | 2. Inspect the participant array
Expected_Result: Each encounter provider appears as an Encounter.participant with the correct individual reference and type

Title: Inbound ADT A01 admit message creates a visit
Preconditions: HL7 v2 inbound interface configured; patient exists; ADT feed enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ADT^A01 admit message with PID and PV1 | 2. Process the message | 3. Verify a visit is created for the patient
Expected_Result: An inbound admit creates an active visit with the admit datetime and patient class from PV1

Title: Inbound ADT A03 discharge message ends the visit
Preconditions: HL7 v2 inbound interface configured; patient has an active visit from a prior A01
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ADT^A03 discharge message referencing the visit | 2. Process the message | 3. Verify the visit stop datetime
Expected_Result: The active visit is closed with the discharge datetime from the A03 message

Title: Malformed PV1 segment in ADT message is rejected and logged
Preconditions: HL7 v2 inbound interface configured
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ADT^A01 with a malformed PV1 segment | 2. Process the message | 3. Review the interface error log
Expected_Result: The message is rejected with an error logged and no partial or corrupt visit is created

Title: Visit row persists patient, type, location, and datetimes correctly
Preconditions: Direct read access to the OpenMRS database; a visit was just created via UI
Test_Steps: 1. Start a visit via the UI | 2. Query the visit table by patient_id | 3. Inspect stored columns
Expected_Result: The visit row stores correct patient_id, visit_type_id, location_id, date_started, and null date_stopped while active

Title: Ending a visit sets date_stopped and preserves encounter linkage
Preconditions: Direct read access to the database; a visit with encounters is ended via UI
Test_Steps: 1. End the visit via UI | 2. Query the visit row for date_stopped | 3. Query encounter rows for visit_id linkage
Expected_Result: date_stopped is populated on the visit row and all encounters retain the correct visit_id foreign key

Title: Voided visit is soft-deleted, not physically removed
Preconditions: Direct read access to the database; user voids a visit via UI
Test_Steps: 1. Void a visit via UI with a reason | 2. Query the visit row | 3. Inspect voided, voided_by, date_voided, void_reason
Expected_Result: The visit row remains with voided=1 and audit columns populated; the row is not physically deleted

Title: Start Visit dialog is fully keyboard navigable
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; using keyboard only with no mouse
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to Start Visit using Tab | 2. Open the dialog | 3. Select a visit type with keyboard | 4. Submit with Enter
Expected_Result: All controls are reachable and operable by keyboard with a visible focus indicator and logical tab order

Title: Visit form fields have programmatic labels for screen readers
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; screen reader (e.g. NVDA) active
Test_Steps: 1. Open the Start Visit form with a screen reader | 2. Tab through each field | 3. Listen to announced labels
Expected_Result: Each field announces a meaningful label and required state, meeting WCAG 2.1 AA name/role/value

Title: Encounter datetime validation against future and visit-window boundaries
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has a visit 09:00 to 17:00 today
Test_Steps: 1. Try encounter datetime one minute before visit start | 2. Try one minute after visit stop | 3. Try future datetime | 4. Try exact stop boundary
Expected_Result: In-window and boundary-valid datetimes are accepted while out-of-window and future datetimes are rejected with clear errors

Title: Start visit with non-ASCII and long patient context handles gracefully
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient with accented name and very long address exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Start a visit | 3. Verify display and persistence of patient context
Expected_Result: The visit is created and the UI renders accented characters and long fields without truncation errors or corruption

Title: Visit note requires a non-empty note or diagnosis
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open Visit Note | 2. Leave note text and diagnosis empty | 3. Save
Expected_Result: A validation warning is shown that the visit note requires content and an empty note is not silently saved

Title: Start a telehealth visit and attach a session link
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician on omiiCARE platform; telehealth visit type configured
Test_Steps: 1. Start a visit and select Telehealth | 2. Generate or attach a session link | 3. Save | 4. Verify the link is accessible from the visit
Expected_Result: A telehealth visit is created with an accessible session link recorded on the visit

Title: Visit completion triggers a billable encounter record
Preconditions: Logged in on omiiCARE platform; billing module enabled; charge codes configured
Test_Steps: 1. Complete a visit with a billable encounter type | 2. End the visit | 3. Open the billing/charges view
Expected_Result: A billable charge is generated and linked to the completed visit with the correct charge code

Title: Block visit note creation when patient consent is not on file
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician on omiiCARE; consent enforcement enabled; patient has no active consent record
Test_Steps: 1. Open an active visit | 2. Attempt to create a visit note | 3. Observe consent enforcement
Expected_Result: The system blocks or warns before recording clinical data without a valid consent on file

Title: Concurrent active visits across two locations are prevented or reconciled
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active visit at Location A
Test_Steps: 1. With the patient active at Location A, attempt to start a visit at Location B | 2. Observe handling
Expected_Result: The system either prevents a second concurrent active visit or applies the documented reconciliation rule without data corruption

Title: Decision table for provider role requirement by encounter type
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; encounter types with differing provider-requirement configs exist
Test_Steps: 1. For each encounter type, attempt to save with zero, one, and multiple providers | 2. Record acceptance against configured rule
Expected_Result: Each encounter type enforces its configured provider requirement consistently per the decision table

Title: Active visits list updates in near real time after starting a visit
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; active visits list open in one session
Test_Steps: 1. Start a new visit for a patient | 2. Return to or refresh the active visits list
Expected_Result: The newly started visit appears in the active visits list reflecting current state
